label: Cervix uteri
quick_take: The cancer that launched this project. CI5 captures the full arc: the clean Pap-era decline in the West; the unmatched Sub-Saharan burden; the East-Asian reversal where urban-China coastal cities have gone from very low to markedly rising (and this is what Shanghai's U-shape looks like). The UK's bimodal / early-peak age curves are a less-discussed fingerprint of HPV that CI5 makes very clear.

findings.title: The classic U-shape in Singapore Indian women
findings.text: 40.4 (vol III) → 3.7 (vol X) → 4.2 (vol XII). 89 % fall, now flat. Same U in New Brunswick (n=276→149), Hawaii Japanese (n=116→37), Hawaii Chinese (n=17→10), Geneva (n=112→58), Hawaii White (n=132→62). The shape is a cervical-cytology story: crashing rates then plateau. Singapore-Indian cases are small in early volumes (n=8–25); the U-shape in Hawaii-Chinese sits on single-digit counts in some vols — the combined signal across six populations makes the pattern robust even where individual subgroups are noisy.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The classic U-shape in Singapore Indian women

findings.title: Uganda Kyadondo is the outlier where the rate kept rising
findings.text: 24.8 (vol I) → 54.3 (vol X) → 48.0 (vol XII). Tripled and hasn't meaningfully retreated.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Uganda Kyadondo is the outlier where the rate kept rising

findings.title: Sub-Saharan African registries are still the world's top
findings.text: Zimbabwe Harare African women 86.7 (vol X) and 75.0 (vol XII); Zimbabwe Bulawayo 89.9 (vol XII); Malawi Blantyre 76.3. All 10–13× the world median. South Africa Eastern Cape 60.8. These registries carry the world's cervix burden in 2020s data.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Sub-Saharan African registries are still the world's top

findings.title: Inland China is the most spectacular rise
findings.text: Jiashan County 1.18 (vol VIII) → 9.89 (vol XII), +738 %. Qidong 3.69 → 13.78 (U-shape). Harbin Nangang 3.03 → 11.24. Wuhan 3.87 → 11.03. Zhongshan 3.54 → 9.98. Beijing 2.30 → 6.15. All rising, and **none of them fit the global "screening-driven fall" narrative** — these are cities where screening is still being rolled out and the underlying HPV-infection cohort wave has yet to peak. This is the story that justifies the whole project and the HTML presentation.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Inland China is the most spectacular rise

findings.title: Hawaii populations collapse together
findings.text: Hawaiian -92 %, Chinese -87 %, Japanese -88 %, Filipino -90 %, Pacific peoples -90 %. One of the cleanest "big-cohort screening" effects anywhere.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Hawaii populations collapse together

findings.title: Early peak in UK women across all sub-registries, vol XI–XII
findings.text: Peak at age band 6 (25–34) in England, West Midlands, South West, East, South East — every large English registry. This is a striking early-age fingerprint that reflects the UK's earlier HPV-infection cohorts plus strong screening above 35 pushing the peak younger.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Early peak in UK women across all sub-registries, vol XI–XII

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in the Netherlands and Denmark
findings.text: a second peak in the 70s. This "screening-leaves-a-tail" pattern is known but CI5 vol VIII–X shows it with large case counts.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in the Netherlands and Denmark

findings.text: Hawaii populations have a pop_warning from vol VII; the magnitude of the collapse is credible but the exact trajectory inside volumes VI–VII should not be over-parsed.
Rising inland-Chinese registries should be checked against HPV-prevalence surveys; a portion of the rise may be ascertainment as registries mature.

findings.title: A Shanghai-plus-inland-China figure showing the coastal fall and inland rise
findings.text: is the core story the HTML hinted at. Add Wuhan and Harbin, and it becomes a paper.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — A Shanghai-plus-inland-China figure showing the coastal fall and inland rise

findings.text: The Sub-Saharan plateau at 50–90 ASR is a policy story, not a surprise — but CI5's 60-year window shows how long the excess has been sustained.
UK's age-peak compression to age band 6 is a clear HPV-era fingerprint that deserves a methods note.
